dependencies: Dependence on supplier relationships for quality products at favorable prices
Approximately 55% of 2024 automotive parts inventories purchased from 10 major suppliers
Approximately 46% of total industrial product purchases in 2024 were made from top 50 strategic suppliers
Supplier consolidation could limit number of suppliers and affect prices
Reliance on vendor return privileges to protect from inventory obsolescence
risks: Labor strikes, shortages and disputes in supply chain
Tariff and customs legislation and enforcement
Financial or operational difficulties at suppliers
Global supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts

regulatory_risks: Restrictions on access to diagnostic tools and repair information imposed by original vehicle manufacturers or governmental regulation
Changes in legislation or government regulations or policies, particularly those relating to taxation and international trade
Changes to existing international trade agreements, blocking of foreign trade, increased protectionism, or imposition of tariffs on foreign goods
Section 232 tariffs on imported products of steel and aluminum
Section 301 tariffs on most imported products from China
New tariffs enacted as of February 2025 that significantly increase tariffs on foreign imports into the United States
Changes in tax laws or interpretation/enforcement of existing tax laws
Compliance with environmental protection regulations
Compliance with product quality standards
Compliance with cybersecurity regulations
Compliance with machine learning and artificial intelligence regulations
Compliance with data privacy regulations
Compliance with building and zoning requirements
Compliance with employment law matters
Laws enacted to reduce greenhouse gas emissions
Mandatory annual reporting requirements related to carbon emissions and other sustainability-related information
Significant increases in fuel economy requirements
New federal or state restrictions on emissions of carbon dioxide
New federal or state incentive programs for vehicles and automobile fuels

Increasing number of laws in various jurisdictions relating to taxes, environmental protection, product quality standards, cybersecurity, machine learning, artificial intelligence, data privacy, building and zoning requirements, and employment law matters

sanction_risks: U.S., NATO members and other countries have instituted sanctions and other penalties against Russia in response to its conflict with Ukraine
Retaliatory measures creating global security concerns
Potential broader military and political conflicts
Disruptions to global automotive supply chains
Adverse changes in international trade policies and relations, including U.S. relations with China
